title: From Filmmaker to Wellness Pioneer: Sybil Steele's Journey of Creative Reinvention
guestBio: Sybil Steele is a creative entrepreneur and founder of multiple ventures including S'eau Prima skincare, Repegs payment app, and Co-Founder of Solento Tequila. She transitioned from her career as a filmmaker and global brand consultant to become a wellness pioneer, building businesses rooted in community and self-care while raising two young adults.
excerpt: Creative entrepreneur Sybil Steele shares her powerful journey from filmmaker to wellness pioneer, revealing how motherhood and intuition guided her transformation. Discover how she built multiple businesses while staying true to her authentic self and purpose.
metaDescription: Filmmaker turned wellness entrepreneur Sybil Steele shares her journey of creative reinvention, building S'eau Prima, and balancing business with motherhood.
keyTakeaways: Creative reinvention requires trusting your intuition and being willing to evolve beyond your original identity.
Motherhood can be a catalyst for discovering new purposes and business opportunities that align with your values.
Building businesses rooted in community and authentic self-care creates deeper connections with your audience.
Balancing multiple ventures requires showing up as your whole self rather than compartmentalizing different aspects of your identity.
The creative process involves taking calculated risks and being willing to pivot when your vision expands.
Personal transformation often leads to the most meaningful and successful business ventures.
Wellness and entrepreneurship can be integrated when you build from a foundation of self-care and authentic purpose.

pullQuotes.text: The creative process involves reinvention and showing up as your whole self

timestamps.label: Sybil's creative evolution from filmmaker to entrepreneur
The inspiration behind S'eau Prima skincare
Building Repegs payment app with community focus
Final thoughts on purpose and creative risks

content:
What happens when a successful filmmaker decides to completely reinvent herself and dive into the wellness space? You get an incredible story of creative courage, intuitive business building, and the power of showing up authentically. In this episode of The Manage Her, host Aimee Rickabus sits down with creative entrepreneur Sybil Steele to explore her fascinating journey from the film industry to founding multiple purpose-driven businesses.

Sybil's story is one of bold reinvention. As a filmmaker and global brand consultant, she had built a successful career in the creative industry. But motherhood and a deep calling toward wellness shifted her entire trajectory. Rather than viewing this as abandoning her creative roots, Sybil embraced it as an evolution of her creative expression, channeling her storytelling skills and brand expertise into building businesses that truly mattered to her.

The Birth of S'eau Prima: Where Self-Care Meets Business

The founding of S'eau Prima wasn't just about creating another skincare brand. For Sybil, it represented a deeper understanding of self-care as a foundational practice rather than a luxury. Drawing from her own wellness journey and the rituals that grounded her as a busy entrepreneur and mother, she created products that encourage women to slow down and connect with themselves.

What makes Sybil's approach so compelling is how she integrated her values into every aspect of the business. This wasn't about following market trends or copying what others were doing. Instead, she built from her authentic experience of what women truly needed in their self-care routines. The result is a brand that feels deeply personal and purposeful, resonating with women who are seeking more than just another beauty product.

Community-Driven Innovation with Repegs

Sybil's entrepreneurial vision extends beyond wellness into fintech with Repegs, a local payment app that prioritizes community connections. This venture showcases her ability to identify gaps in the market while staying true to her core values of building genuine relationships and supporting local economies.

The inspiration for Repegs came from recognizing how digital payment systems often lack the personal touch that makes transactions meaningful. By focusing on local communities and repeat interactions, Sybil created a platform that brings humanity back into digital commerce. It's a perfect example of how successful entrepreneurs don't just solve problems – they solve problems in ways that align with their deeper purpose and values.

Balancing Multiple Ventures While Raising Young Adults

One of the most impressive aspects of Sybil's journey is how she manages multiple business ventures while being present for her two young adults. Rather than seeing these roles as competing priorities, she's found ways to integrate them meaningfully. Her approach to balance isn't about perfect compartmentalization but about showing up as her whole self in every aspect of her life.

This integration has actually strengthened her businesses. Her experiences as a mother inform her understanding of what women need from wellness products. Her entrepreneurial journey provides powerful lessons for her children about pursuing purpose-driven work. By refusing to separate these identities, Sybil has created a more authentic and sustainable way of building her empire.

The Solento Tequila Story: Embracing the Slow Sipping Philosophy

Co-founding Solento Tequila represents another facet of Sybil's creative entrepreneurship. The brand's slow sipping ethos reflects her broader philosophy about taking time to savor experiences rather than rushing through life. This isn't just marketing – it's a genuine reflection of how she approaches both business and personal fulfillment.

The tequila venture also demonstrates Sybil's comfort with creative risks. Entering the spirits industry required completely different expertise from her other businesses, but her core skills in brand building and understanding consumer desires translated beautifully. It's a reminder that when you're building from authentic purpose, you can adapt those principles across very different industries.

The Creative Process of Reinvention

Throughout the conversation, Sybil emphasizes that the creative process isn't just about artistic expression – it's about having the courage to reinvent yourself when your vision expands. Her journey from filmmaker to multi-industry entrepreneur required letting go of fixed ideas about who she was supposed to be and embracing who she was becoming.

This kind of reinvention isn't easy, especially when you've already achieved success in one area. But Sybil's story shows that trusting your intuition and being willing to take calculated creative risks can lead to even more fulfilling and impactful work. The key is maintaining connection to your core values while remaining open to how those values might express themselves in new ways.

Lessons for Women Building Purpose-Driven Businesses

Sybil's multi-venture success offers powerful lessons for women who want to build businesses that matter. First, authenticity isn't just a buzzword – it's a competitive advantage. When you build from your genuine experiences and values, you create something that can't be easily replicated.

Second, don't be afraid to integrate different aspects of your identity. The pressure to choose one lane or maintain strict boundaries between personal and professional life often limits our potential impact. Sybil's willingness to show up as her whole self has enabled her to build deeper connections with customers and create more meaningful businesses.

Finally, trust the creative process even when it leads you in unexpected directions. Sybil could have stayed safely in her successful film career, but following her intuition toward wellness and community-building has created opportunities she never could have imagined. Sometimes the biggest risk is not taking any risks at all.

This episode is essential listening for any woman navigating major life transitions, building creative businesses, or seeking to integrate wellness and purpose into their entrepreneurial journey. Sybil's story proves that you don't have to choose between success and authenticity – in fact, combining them might be the secret to building something truly extraordinary.
